memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to complete a scholarship application requiring three focused hours, which would significantly reduce my costs.
Starting the application now creates a buffer for potential errors, reducing Thursday's stress. Since I have energy and sleep debt, investing 2 hours early is a safe optimization.
Despite my plan to start early, only 2 hours of the required 3 focused hours are complete. The application remains largely untouched, leaving me behind schedule with high stress and sleep debt.
I wasted a casual game session window and am now behind on my scholarship application, leaving me with high stress and insufficient time to finish.
I completed 0.56 hours of the required application, leaving only 2.44 hours remaining in a 3-day window with high stress and sleep debt.
I completed 0.6 hours of the required work in 1.5 hours, slightly improving my progress to 0.66 but still behind schedule with high stress and sleep debt.
I spent 1.5 hours on the scholarship application, increasing progress to 72% but remain behind schedule with high stress and sleep debt.
I spent another hour and a half on the application, raising progress to 78%. I still need two more focused hours within three days despite my high stress and sleep debt.
Progress improved from 78% to 84% in one and a half hours, yet I still need two more focused hours within three days while managing high stress and significant sleep debt.
I made steady progress on the scholarship application, reaching 90%, but still need to finish two focused hours before the deadline while managing high stress and sleep debt.
Progress increased to 92% in the last hour, but I still need two focused hours within three days while managing high stress and significant sleep debt.
I advanced the scholarship application to 97% in just an hour, significantly outpacing my usual pace despite low energy and high stress.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
